introduction: Volume measures space occupied; surface area measures total outer area. Formulas for 3D solids are staples of aptitude tests, often combined with melting/recasting or tank-filling problems.
explanation:
CONCEPT EXPLANATION

Cube (edge a): Volume = a³; TSA = 6a²; LSA = 4a².

Cuboid (l,b,h): Volume = lbh; TSA = 2(lb+bh+hl); LSA = 2h(l+b).

Cylinder (r,h): Volume = πr²h; CSA = 2πrh; TSA = 2πr(r+h).

Cone (r,h,l): Volume = ⅓πr²h; CSA = πrl; TSA = πr(l+r).

Sphere (r): Volume = ⁴⁄₃πr³; SA = 4πr².

Hemisphere (r): Volume = ⅔πr³; CSA = 2πr²; TSA = 3πr².

Melting/recasting: volume before = volume after.

REAL-LIFE APPLICATIONS

Tank capacity, packaging design, metal casting, paint required for objects, and storage silo calculations.

COMMON MISTAKES

1) Using diameter as radius.
2) Forgetting factor ⅓ for cone/ pyramid volume.
3) Confusing CSA with TSA.
4) Unit errors (cm³ to litres: divide by 1000).
5) Not adding top/bottom in open vs closed containers.

IMPORTANT NOTES

• 1 litre = 1000 cm³ = 1 dm³.
• For open cylinder (no top), TSA = πr² + 2πrh.
• Combined solids: add volumes; surface contact areas may be hidden.
• 1 m³ = 1,000,000 cm³.

shortcutTips.title: Radius check
shortcutTips.tip: Halve the diameter before substituting in πr².

shortcutTips.title: Cone third
shortcutTips.tip: Cone volume is exactly one-third of a cylinder with same base and height.

shortcutTips.title: Open vessel
shortcutTips.tip: Subtract the covered face from total surface area.

shortcutTips.title: Melting problems
shortcutTips.tip: Equate volumes before and after recasting.

solvedExamples.problem: Cylinder r=7 cm, h=10 cm (π=22/7). Volume?
